Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Valerie Thomelia Williams, Petitioner in Pro Per.
Jason Floyd Williams, Petitioner in Pro Per (via Zoom).
Omari C. French, Proposed Administrator.

The Court called the matter for hearing on two competing petitions for letters of administration. The Court inquired into the decedent's estate planning documents and confirmed that no valid trust had been executed; therefore, the decedent died intestate.

Jason Williams stated that he was in agreement with the nomination of Pastor Omari French as administrator, consistent with the decedent's previously stated wishes.

The Court confirmed that both parties agree to waive bond. Mr. French indicated that no additional fees will be sought, and the intent is to maximize distribution of the estate equally between Jason and Valerie Williams.

The Court discussed procedural deficiencies in the pending petition, which Mr. French and Ms. Williams agreed to correct. Ms. Williams requested a 30-day continuance, to allow time to address deficiencies. Jason Williams agreed to withdraw his competing petition, with the understanding that Valerie Williams was not seeking to be appointed administrator herself.

Following discussion, the Court continued Ms. Williams' petition to July 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Jason Williams' petition for letters of administration was taken off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Continued To Jul-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ray Robinson appeared via zoom for and with Jason Williams (petitioner).
Valerie Williams and Dejon Scott Miller appeared in person (objectors).

Discussion was held regarding the real property, that there is a reverse mortgage of $900,000.00 on the house, that Ms. Williams is living in the house with her adult son, that they are not paying rent, and that Ms. Williams expects to move out of the house before June 01, 2025. Mr. Robinson stated that his client is bondable. The Court suggested that Ms. Williams file a competing petition, and that it should be filed and served by May 21, 2025. The matter is continued to June 18, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.
